sections.paragraph:
ὄστρακον, ου, τό (Hom. Hymns et al.; SIG 1168, 82; 86 [IV b.c.]; POxy 234 II, 3; 1450, 4; O. Wilck II, 1152, 5; LXX; JosAs 13:8; Philo, Somn. 2, 57; Ar. 4, 3) baked clay, pottery of polytheists’ deities ὁ δὲ ὄστρακον another is a piece of pottery Dg 2:2. Collectively ὄς. λεπτόν little pieces of broken pottery Hs 9, 10, 1 (difft. EGoodspeed, JBL 73, ’54, 85f).—DELG. M-M s.v. ὀστράκινος.

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

  1. Wilck O. Wilck = UWilcken, ed., Griechische Ostraka aus Ägypten u. Nubien—List 4

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

JosAs JosAs = Joseph and Aseneth—Lists 2, 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Ar. Ar. = Aristides, apologist, II a.d.—List 5

Dg Dg = Letter of Diognetus, attributed to an unidentifiable apologist; II a.d.—List 1

Hs Hs = Similitudes

difft. difft. = different(ly)

JBL JBL = Journal of Biblical Literature—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 730
